When providing services to clients, is it possible that your Goods/Services may be sourced or produced domestically and potentially internationally?\n\n*As you select each of the countries you source Goods/Services from, you’ll be required to confirm the type of Good/Service & Sourcing Arrangement, see example below. *

Can your company confirm there are processes in place to ‘map’ your supply chain, increasing its transparency / visibility and confirming who provides / delivers specific services to your business?
Processes being developed for implementation within twelve months (Ongoing development work)
Company has undertaken partial mapping of the supply chain only

Has the mapping of your supply chain occurred in your domestic supply chain?
Has the mapping of your supply chain occurred in your international supply chain? \n

Does your company have a policy statement, program or management system that confirms a commitment to preventing modern slavery, forced labor and human trafficking within the company’s operations and supply chain?

Your company may have policy statements or working documentation that contains an integrated approach as opposed to independent, separate documents. If the integrated documentation references anti-slavery policies and contains a commitment to address risks using the combined programs, it is acceptable to upload the integrated documentation as part of your supporting evidence.
Please upload a policy statement / document related to the prevention of modern slavery, forced labor and human trafficking:
Does this policy statement cover anti-slavery practices?
Can your company confirm that it complies with national and international legislation relating to modern slavery, forced labor, and human trafficking?
Does this statement and all of the prohibited practices relating to forced labor extend to subcontractors, agents and other third-parties involved with the delivery of company services?
Does the management commitment  confirm, that the following classification of modern slavery and forced labor are prohibited within the business and its supply chain?
Please explain why there is no policy / statement currently in place.
Company believes this policy is not required based on current scope of work
Company does not believe it is big enough to justify this category/type of policy
Current company management programs currently under review
Company currently developing modern slavery statement/policy and it will be implemented within the next 12 months
Company currently developing modern slavery statement/policy and it will be implemented within the next 6 months
Other: Additional information, details, answers, or documents entered under this question may be subject to verification / examination, but will not be considered in the overall ESG Assessment score

Has the company appointed a senior management representative responsible for the implementation, enforcement and compliance of the company policy statement / management program regarding the prevention of modern slavery and forced labor?

Can the company demonstrate that there are management systems and company processes in place that will monitor your operations and evaluate all categories of suppliers post contract award to ensure it mitigates the potential of the introduction of slavery into your supply chain? (All activities from supply of materials, provider of a specific service etc.)\n
If the company has implemented management controls as confirmed in part (a), are the controls applied to all levels / tiers within your supply chain?
If you answer no, please advise what the restrictions are e.g. ‘We only assess all tier 1 suppliers.'
Please confirm what processes, associated with the question above, are used:
Audit / Inspection program: Pre contract evaluation
Audit / Inspection program: During contract delivery
Formal reporting in event an ethical / human rights issue is identified
Investigation procedure in the event an ethical / human rights issue is identified
Company currently developing processes for implementation within next twelve months
If none of the above, please specify in the free text box what actions are taken:
Upload supporting evidence based on checked options

Does the company participate within any 3rd party / independent ethical sourcing program that is relevant to human rights, corporate social responsibility e.g. United Nations Global Compact, SA8000, SEDEX, BSCI?
Currently evaluating if this type of programme will assist the business in reducing the risk of modern slavery
Does your company undertake or use any form of evaluation audit / program that analyses potential human rights issues within your operations or when engaging with suppliers?\n
Does your company utilize a 3rd party program / independent audit?
Indicate below the audit timelines utilized:
Specific performance criteria in place to monitor effectiveness of audit systems, processes or procedures
Post contract delivery / Post work evaluation
Report of a human rights violation or adverse publicity about the supplier
Specific performance criteria in place to monitor effectiveness of audit systems, processes or procedures

If your company uses the services of subcontractors, labor only companies or labor from employment businesses/agencies on any of your worksites / premises, can you confirm that checks are in place to confirm they have controls in place and the systems have been assessed and validated to ensure they are not using workers illegally, and the workers being used are not forced to work or are subject to bonded labor?
Upload supporting evidence to confirm how this process is assured before using these workers

As part of the due diligence process when using this category of worker, does your company ensure that the third party being used undertake eligibility checks on their workforce to confirm they can work legally in your primary country of operation?
Upload supporting evidence to confirm how this process is assured before using these workers

As part of the company procurement process for sourcing of suppliers/third parties, does the company utilize any form of risk assessment within their operations or when engaging with a new supplier / third party that considers the potential risks of slavery, human trafficking, forced labor and underage labor?\n\n
Company currently developing process to be implemented within twelve months
Is your Company currently developing a process to be implemented within twelve months?
Does the risk assessment process include control measures to confirm foreseeable risks have been reduced to their lowest level?

As part of the supplier engagement process, do formal contracts / arrangements placed with suppliers contain obligations placed upon them that prevent the supplier from outsourcing manufacturing to other 3rd parties who have not been subject to any form of client evaluation?
Does your company currently provide training / briefing guides on how to recognize issues that may indicate the presence of: Slavery; Human trafficking; Forced labor and Underage labor?
Is the Company currently developing process to be implemented within twelve months?
Please confirm who the training is provided to:
Procurement personnel (Specific to engagement with suppliers & work activity / service)
Forms part of contract with supply chain (issued as guidance or contract mandate)

If an issue relating to slavery / human trafficking / forced labor and underage labor is suspected or identified within your operations or supply chain, has a mitigation / remediation plan been prepared and implemented to address the potential issues?
Is the company currently developing a process to be implemented within twelve months?
Is the remediation process different if the issue identified is with an international supplier?
Has the company or any company within the supply chain been found to have identified issues around modern slavery or forced labor?
Issues relating to modern slavery and forced labor were identified:

Found within primary country of operation
